commands.review_summary:
Adds `.github/workflows/deploy-website.yml` to build `website/` with pnpm and deploy `website/dist` to GitHub Pages on pushes to `main`.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the PR sets `VITE_BASE=/vox/`, but current `website/vite.config.ts` has no `base` setting and does not read that environment variable. No live Pages run was available in the PR body to verify the deployed result.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-visible proof and maintainer review after the workflow/config fixes; automation cannot supply the missing real Pages evidence.

target.title: ci: add GitHub Pages deploy workflow for website
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `.github/workflows/deploy-website.yml` to build and deploy the `website/` directory to GitHub Pages on every push to `main` that touches `website/**`
- Builds with `VITE_BASE=/vox/` so Vite generates asset paths rooted at `steipete.github.io/vox/`
- Uses `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3` + `actions/deploy-pages@v4` in a two-job pipeline (build → deploy) with the `pages` concurrency group to prevent overlapping deployments

## Required one-time setup

Before the first deploy can run, a repo admin must enable Pages in:
**Settings → Pages → Source → GitHub Actions**

## Notes

The `website/vite.config.ts` base-path change (`base: process.env.VITE_BASE ?? "/"`) is part of the `fix/truncate-audio-bounds` PR and must land on `main` alongside the website source for the deploy to produce correct asset URLs. This workflow file can merge in any order — it will simply wait for the next `website/**` push to trigger.

## Test plan

- [ ] Enable Pages source in repo settings (Settings → Pages → GitHub Actions)
- [ ] Merge `fix/truncate-audio-bounds` (brings `website/` + `vite.config.ts` base-path fix)
- [ ] Confirm workflow triggers and site is live at `https://steipete.github.io/vox/`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: joevenner
